title: Core Ethical Dilemmas in AI Public Policy
description: A flowchart illustrating the key ethical problems in AI policy, their interdependencies, and the resulting policy challenges they create.
relationships: Bias in algorithms leads to a lack of Fairness, creating a need for policy on Transparency to ensure Explainability of outcomes. Without this, assigning Accountability and Liability becomes a major challenge. Widespread data collection for AI raises Privacy and Surveillance concerns, which conflict with the need for data to improve systems. Ensuring Safety and Security is paramount, but the pursuit of Autonomy and reduced Human Control can create new safety risks, requiring policy to define acceptable levels of automation.
